title: modenanalyse_2fe2s: Mode-resolved bonding reorganization analysis for [2Fe-2S] iron-sulfur clusters from DFT frequency calculations
description:
Python program for mode-resolved bonding reorganization analysis of [2Fe-2S] iron-sulfur clusters from Gaussian-16 or ORCA-6 frequency calculations. For each vibrational mode and each bonding channel (Fe-Fe, Fe-N, Fe-S, N-H, H…acceptor), computes thermally scaled bond modulations ΔrX and the associated reorganization contributions λX = ½ μ ω2 (ΔrX)2 in Marcus-Hush form, with a special reaction-coordinate mode for hydrogen bonds (PCET-relevant).

Produces frequency-resolved spectra MX(ω) and cumulative ΛX(ω), a D2h symmetry-coordinate decomposition (Kingsbury & Senge), plus UMAP embedding with HDBSCAN clustering of the mode feature matrix.

Main application: NRVS band identification and system comparisons for Rieske-type proteins and mitoNEET. Multi-cluster systems (dimers, multi-domain constructs) are supported as a first-class feature.

Validation: Tested against mitoNEET-H87C QM/MM Hessian (Cys4 ligation, 422 atoms, 1266 modes, 11 UMAP clusters) with characteristic reorganization values ΛFeFe ≈ 29 cm-1, ΛFeS ≈ 338 cm-1.

creators.name: Knauer, Lukas
creators.affiliation: AG Schünemann, Department of Physics, RPTU Kaiserslautern-Landau

Hammes-Schiffer S, Soudackov AV. Proton-coupled electron transfer in solution, proteins, and electrochemistry. J. Phys. Chem. B 112, 14108 (2008). DOI: 10.1021/jp805876e
Huang K, Rhys A. Theory of light absorption and non-radiative transitions in F-centres. Proc. R. Soc. Lond. A 204, 406 (1950).
Kingsbury CJ, Senge MO. Symmetry-coordinate structural decomposition for [2Fe-2S] clusters. Chem. Sci. 15, 13638 (2024).
Gee LB, Pelmenschikov V, Mons C, Mishra N, Wang H, Yoda Y, Tamasaku K, Golinelli-Cohen MP, Cramer SP. Vibrational characterization of mitoNEET. Biochemistry 60, 2419-2424 (2021).
